Fresher:
- Understand the basic layout of the Cocos Creator editor, can create, open, and save projects.
-  Familiar with the scene editor, hierarchy panel, and inspector. 
- Able to add basic game objects (sprites, labels, etc.) to the scene.
-  Understand the concept of nodes and their transformable properties (position, scale, rotation). 
- Basic asset management (importing, using, and organizing assets)
- Basic UI creation and manipulation using the editor
Junior:
- Proficient in using the scene editor to create complex game layouts. 
- Comfortable with different component types and their properties. (Canvas, Camera etc..)
- Able to use the prefab system effectively. 
- Understand and utilize the physics engine for basic interactions. 
- Intermediate asset management (creating atlases, using different asset types).
Mid:
- Deep understanding of the editor's features (batch memory) and workflows. 
- Able to optimize scenes for performance. 
- Proficient in creating and managing complex UI systems.
- Able to create custom editor tools or extensions. 
- Deep understanding Cocos build process and able to build multi platforms: web desktop, mobile, native (Android, iOS)
Senior:
- Expert-level knowledge of the Cocos Creator editor and its architecture. 
- Able to develop advanced custom editor tools and workflows. 
- Deep understanding of the rendering pipeline and its implications for editor workflow.

Fresher:
- Able to write simple scripts to control game objects & UI Components. 
- Familiar with CCClass and CCComponent. 
- Understand the component lifecycle (onLoad, start, update etc..). 
- Able to access and modify node properties through scripts. 
- Basic event handling.
Junior:
- Able to create custom Cocos UI components. 
- Solid understanding of the component lifecycle and how to use it effectively. 
- Experience with different types of events and event handling. 
- Basic understanding of the Collision System or physics engine API.
Mid:
- Deep understanding of the scripting API. 
- Proficient in using the  Collision System or physics engine API (VP3) or Rendering pipeline (VP1) etc..
- Able to design and implement complex game feature using Cocos
- Experience with debugging and profiling scripts.
- Contribute to the improvement of scripting performance and best practices
Senior:
- Able to develop advanced custom components and frameworks. 
- Deep understanding of the engine's architecture and its implications for scripting.

Fresher:
- Basic understanding of animation principles (timing, easing)
- Use CCAction for simple animations
- Understand the Tween system and its basic usage (Tween properties of game objects)
- Basic understanding of Spine integration and usage (Import and use Spine animations in Cocos Creator)
- Control Spine animations through scripting via Spine API
Junior:
- Proficient in using Tween and/or CCAction for complex animations and transitions 
- Understand animation curves and easing functions
- Proficient in using Spine for skeletal animations 
- Quản lí thêm/xoá khi sử dụng tween
- Spine event listener
Mid:
- Advanced animation techniques (timelines, animation blending, state machines) 
- Optimize animation performance and memory usage
- Advanced Spine features (skins, attachments, IK, dressing etc...)
Senior:
- Deep understanding of Tween + CCAction internals and limitations( nên biết từ mid)
- Optimize Spine animation performance

Fresher:
- Variables, data types, operators, control flow 
- Basic understanding of functions and objects
Junior:
- Prototypes, closures, asynchronous programming 
- Working with events and event listeners
-  Basic understanding of TypeScript types, interfaces, and classes
Mid:
- Advanced JavaScript concepts (e.g., promises, async/await, generators, iterators, modules)
- Proficient in TypeScript, using advanced features like generics, decorators, union and intersection types, conditional types 
- Experience with advanced scripting techniques (e.g., inheritance, polymorphism).
Senior:
- Deep understanding of JavaScript internals (e.g., event loop, memory management) 
- Meta-programming and advanced techniques
- Deep understanding of TypeScript's type system and compiler 
- Architecting large-scale projects using TypeScript 
- Contributing to TypeScript definitions and tooling 
- Advanced type manipulation and metaprogramming in TypeScript

Fresher:

Junior:
- Write clean, efficient, and maintainable code 
- Implement basic unit tests
Mid:
- Write well-documented and testable code 
- Perform code reviews and contribute to code quality

Senior:
- Establish coding standards and best practices 
- Develop tools for code analysis and testing 
- Advocate for code quality and maintainability 
- Lead and mentor on code quality practices

Fresher: - Basic understanding of client-server architecture.
Junior:

- Understands basic WebSocket features (e.g., sending/receiving messages, basic connection management, fish movement synchronization).
Mid:
- Comfortable working with WebSockets for real-time communication.
- Handles network data (serialization, deserialization).
- Can use network debugging tools.
Senior: - Experienced in developing and implementing robust and scalable networking solutions (reliable message delivery, synchronization, handling latency).

Senior: N/A

Fresher:

Junior:

Senior:

Senior:

Senior:

Deep understanding of memory management and garbage collection.
